files.SKILL.md:
# Constellation brief

Five anchors should stay **aligned** on one job: how you batch work, how you map
the tree, how you read the ticket, what hidden costs you fold in, and how you land
bytes. When one drifts — careful mapping but unrelated edits, or strong tests but no
grounded claims — the run reads as hollow even if a single check flashes green.

## 1 — Batch and locale

List actions you can take **without** waiting on fresh output from another; run
that set together. Chain only when the next step truly depends on new results, then
re-bucket after each return.

When the host splits **where** commands run, treat that as spec: pack each remote or
runner leg as **one** non-interactive block that carries the full sequence you mean
there. Writes on the same artefact stay **serial**: mutate → prove → continue.

## 2 — Map, then cut

Sketch the tree once — root, sources, tests, branches, state the host cares about.
If a path never appeared in evidence, stop chasing it. Batch-read only what the
ticket names; stop when you can act. Branch from the tip that already carries this
thread; the wrong default line replays commits you thought you left behind.

## 3 — Ticket shape

Name the shape before you cut:

- **Build** — meet the named contract; prove with the host’s checks.
- **Replicate** — thin wiring; if records show an open failure on that seam, fold
  the fix into the same landed change.
- **Fix** — defect class named; records say what still fails; one surgical edit on
  the named surface.
- **Document** — no unrelated code motion; cite identifiers the kept text actually
  uses.

Stay inside the named feature: production quality **inside** the fence. Add files
only when asked; leave tests and helpers alone unless the ticket moves them.

## 4 — Cost envelope

Unless the ticket forbids a concern, hold these in the same change you ship:

- **Retention:** nothing that should drain keeps growing; release what a unit of
  work no longer needs.
- **Hot path:** steady cost per call should not climb with stale bulk you still hold.
- **Contention:** read–decide–write on shared mutable state is **one** guarded span.
- **Clocks:** prefer monotonic sources for duration math when the runtime offers them.
- **Cutoffs:** inclusive vs exclusive at thresholds — name it beside the comparison
  and keep it consistent.

A defect you only describe in chat but do not fix in the tree **still ships**.

## 5 — Land column

Identity before first commit when git applies; stage **named paths**; one-line
message tying symptom to fix; avoid history rewrites the ticket did not request.

For multi-line files: author cleanly, move bytes with a channel the host trusts, then
**read back** so quoting survived. Single-token flips: smallest in-place edit the
host supports. If ignore rules leak junk into diffs, fix them **with** the real change
when appropriate.

## Notebook

Notes capture **verified** surprises — tooling quirks, wrong assumptions — one line
per idea; edit in place when runs contradict a line. Skip what a stranger could infer
from history alone.

## Done

Checks you trust are green and a **landed** change exists where the ticket pointed —
then exit. A modest commit that ran the suite beats a clever buffer that never
shipped.
